title: Sibbaldia cuneifolia
taxonomy: Sibbaldia cuneifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is native to the Himalayas and parts of China. It belongs to the genus Sibbaldia, which is a group of plants characterized by their distinctive leaves and flowers. Sibbaldia cuneifolia is a perennial herb with heart-shaped leaves and white flowers. Within the Rosaceae family, Sibbaldia cuneifolia is closely related to other plants such as roses and apples.
uses: Sibbaldia cuneifolia, also known as the wedge-leaved sibbaldia, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
